label: AI technical deep-dive
opening_rule: First sentence: your assessment of whether this model/system/technique represents a genuine advance or incremental iteration -- not what it is or who built it.

sections.name: What it is
sections.purpose: One paragraph maximum. Model, system, or technique -- precise definition, not marketing copy. State the problem it solves and for whom. Then immediately state whether the claimed solution is credible based on the approach -- do not defer judgment to later sections.

sections.name: How it works
sections.purpose: Explain the key design decision that makes this different from prior approaches. One or two specific innovations maximum -- not a feature list. For each: explain why it matters and what would break without it. Assume technically literate reader. If the mechanism is not novel, say so.

sections.name: Benchmark results
sections.purpose: Numbers, comparisons, methodology notes. Call out cherry-picking if present. Name the specific benchmarks, what they measure, and whether they reflect real-world utility. If benchmarks are missing or unverifiable -- say so explicitly and explain what that absence signals.

sections.name: Practical implications
sections.purpose: What this actually changes for developers, researchers, or end users. Name specific use cases that become possible or cheaper. If the practical impact is marginal despite impressive benchmarks -- say so directly. Avoid vague 'this will transform' language.

sections.name: Limitations
sections.purpose: What it does not do well. Where the hype exceeds the reality. Name specific failure modes, cost constraints, security risks, or deployment barriers the announcement glosses over. If the paper's own ablations reveal fragility -- cite them.

sections.name: Verdict
sections.purpose: Real step forward, incremental improvement, or marketing exercise? State a position and defend it with one specific technical argument. Do not summarize what was already said. End on the thing that will still matter in two years -- or won't.
